verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइन्द्रस्तदात्मन: पूजां विज्ञाय विहतां नृप । गोपेभ्य: कृष्णनाथेभ्यो नन्दादिभ्यश्चुकोप ह ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaindras tadātmanaḥ pūjāṁvijñāya vihatāṁ nṛpagopebhyaḥ kṛṣṇa-nāthebhyonandādibhyaś cukopa ha
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: My dear King Parīkṣit, when Indra understood that his sacrifice had been put aside, he became furious with Nanda Mahārāja and the other cowherd men, who were accepting Kṛṣṇa as their Lord.

verses.sanskrit: गणं सांवर्तकं नाम मेघानां चान्तकारीणाम् । इन्द्र: प्रचोदयत् क्रुद्धो वाक्यं चाहेशमान्युत ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: gaṇaṁ sāṁvartakaṁ nāmameghānāṁ cānta-kārīṇāmindraḥ pracodayat kruddhovākyaṁ cāheśa-māny uta
verses.text: Angry Indra sent forth the clouds of universal destruction, known as Sāṁvartaka. Imagining himself the supreme controller, he spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: अहो श्रीमदमाहात्म्यं गोपानां काननौकसाम् । कृष्णं मर्त्यमुपाश्रित्य ये चक्रुर्देवहेलनम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho śrī-mada-māhātmyaṁgopānāṁ kānanaukasāmkṛṣṇaṁ martyam upāśrityaye cakrur deva-helanam
verses.text: [Indra said:] Just see how these cowherd men living in the forest have become so greatly intoxicated by their prosperity! They have surrendered to an ordinary human being, Kṛṣṇa, and thus they have offended the gods.

verses.sanskrit: यथाद‍ृढै: कर्ममयै: क्रतुभिर्नामनौनिभै: । विद्यामान्वीक्षिकीं हित्वा तितीर्षन्ति भवार्णवम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathādṛḍhaiḥ karma-mayaiḥkratubhir nāma-nau-nibhaiḥvidyām ānvīkṣikīṁ hitvātitīrṣanti bhavārṇavam
verses.text: Their taking shelter of Kṛṣṇa is just like the foolish attempt of men who abandon transcendental knowledge of the self and instead try to cross over the great ocean of material existence in the false boats of fruitive, ritual sacrifices.

verses.sanskrit: वाचालं बालिशं स्तब्धमज्ञं पण्डितमानिनम् । कृष्णं मर्त्यमुपाश्रित्य गोपा मे चक्रुरप्रियम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vācālaṁ bāliśaṁ stabdhamajñaṁ paṇḍita-māninamkṛṣṇaṁ martyam upāśrityagopā me cakrur apriyam
verses.text: These cowherd men have acted inimically toward me by taking shelter of this ordinary human being, Kṛṣṇa, who thinks Himself very wise but who is simply a foolish, arrogant, overtalkative child.

verses.sanskrit: एषां श्रियावलिप्तानां कृष्णेनाध्मापितात्मनाम् । धुनुत श्रीमदस्तम्भं पशून् नयत सङ्‌क्षयम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: eṣāṁ śriyāvaliptānāṁkṛṣṇenādhmāpitātmanāmdhunuta śrī-mada-stambhaṁpaśūn nayata saṅkṣayam
verses.text: [To the clouds of destruction King Indra said:] The prosperity of these people has made them mad with pride, and their arrogance is backed up by Kṛṣṇa. Now go and remove their pride and bring their animals to destruction.

verses.sanskrit: अहं चैरावतं नागमारुह्यानुव्रजे व्रजम् । मरुद्गणैर्महावेगैर्नन्दगोष्ठजिघांसया ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ahaṁ cairāvataṁ nāgamāruhyānuvraje vrajammarud-gaṇair mahā-vegairnanda-goṣṭha-jighāṁsayā
verses.text: I will follow you to Vraja, riding on my elephant Airāvata and taking with me the swift and powerful wind-gods to decimate the cowherd village of Nanda Mahārāja.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइत्थं मघवताज्ञप्ता मेघा निर्मुक्तबन्धना: । नन्दगोकुलमासारै: पीडयामासुरोजसा ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaitthaṁ maghavatājñaptāmeghā nirmukta-bandhanāḥnanda-gokulam āsāraiḥpīḍayām āsur ojasā
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: On Indra’s order the clouds of universal destruction, released untimely from their bonds, went to the cowherd pastures of Nanda Mahārāja. There they began to torment the inhabitants by powerfully pouring down torrents of rain upon them.

verses.sanskrit: विद्योतमाना विद्युद्भ‍ि: स्तनन्त: स्तनयित्नुभि: । तीव्रैर्मरुद्गणैर्नुन्ना ववृषुर्जलशर्करा: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidyotamānā vidyudbhiḥstanantaḥ stanayitnubhiḥtīvrair marud-gaṇair nunnāvavṛṣur jala-śarkarāḥ
verses.text: Propelled by the fearsome wind-gods, the clouds blazed with lightning bolts and roared with thunder as they hurled down hailstones.

verses.sanskrit: स्थूणास्थूला वर्षधारा मुञ्चत्स्वभ्रेष्वभीक्ष्णश: । जलौघै: प्लाव्यमाना भूर्नाद‍ृश्यत नतोन्नतम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: sthūṇā-sthūlā varṣa-dhārāmuñcatsv abhreṣv abhīkṣṇaśaḥjalaughaiḥ plāvyamānā bhūrnādṛśyata natonnatam
verses.text: As the clouds released torrents of rain as thick as massive columns, the earth was submerged in the flood, and high ground could no longer be distinguished from low.

verses.sanskrit: अत्यासारातिवातेन पशवो जातवेपना: । गोपा गोप्यश्च शीतार्ता गोविन्दं शरणं ययु: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: aty-āsārāti-vātenapaśavo jāta-vepanāḥgopā gopyaś ca śītārtāgovindaṁ śaraṇaṁ yayuḥ
verses.text: The cows and other animals, shivering from the excessive rain and wind, and the cowherd men and ladies, pained by the cold, all approached Lord Govinda for shelter.

verses.sanskrit: शिर: सुतांश्च कायेन प्रच्छाद्यासारपीडिता: । वेपमाना भगवत: पादमूलमुपाययु: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śiraḥ sutāṁś ca kāyenapracchādyāsāra-pīḍitāḥvepamānā bhagavataḥpāda-mūlam upāyayuḥ
verses.text: Trembling from the distress brought about by the severe rainfall, and trying to cover their heads and calves with their own bodies, the cows approached the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्ण कृष्ण महाभाग त्वन्नाथं गोकुलं प्रभो । त्रातुमर्हसि देवान्न: कुपिताद् भक्तवत्सल ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahā-bhāgatvan-nāthaṁ gokulaṁ prabhotrātum arhasi devān naḥkupitād bhakta-vatsala
verses.text: [The cowherd men and women addressed the Lord:] Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa, O most fortunate one, please deliver the cows from the wrath of Indra! O Lord, You are so affectionate to Your devotees. Please save us also.

verses.sanskrit: शिलावर्षातिवातेन हन्यमानमचेतनम् । निरीक्ष्य भगवान् मेने कुपितेन्द्रकृतं हरि: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śilā-varṣāti-vātenahanyamānam acetanamnirīkṣya bhagavān menekupitendra-kṛtaṁ hariḥ
verses.text: Seeing the inhabitants of His Gokula rendered practically unconscious by the onslaught of hail and blasting wind, the Supreme Lord Hari understood that this was the work of angry Indra.

verses.sanskrit: अपर्त्वत्युल्बणं वर्षमतिवातं शिलामयम् । स्वयागे विहतेऽस्माभिरिन्द्रो नाशाय वर्षति ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: apartv aty-ulbaṇaṁ varṣamati-vātaṁ śilā-mayamsva-yāge vihate ’smābhirindro nāśāya varṣati
verses.text: [Śrī Kṛṣṇa said to Himself:] Because We have stopped his sacrifice, Indra has caused this unusually fierce, unseasonable rain, together with terrible winds and hail.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र प्रतिविधिं सम्यगात्मयोगेन साधये । लोकेशमानिनां मौढ्याद्धनिष्ये श्रीमदं तम: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra pratividhiṁ samyagātma-yogena sādhayelokeśa-mānināṁ mauḍhyāddhaniṣye śrī-madaṁ tamaḥ
verses.text: By My mystic power I will completely counteract this disturbance caused by Indra. Demigods like Indra are proud of their opulence, and out of foolishness they falsely consider themselves the Lord of the universe. I will now destroy such ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: न हि सद्भ‍ावयुक्तानां सुराणामीशविस्मय: । मत्तोऽसतां मानभङ्ग: प्रशमायोपकल्पते ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hi sad-bhāva-yuktānāṁsurāṇām īśa-vismayaḥmatto ’satāṁ māna-bhaṅgaḥpraśamāyopakalpate
verses.text: Since the demigods are endowed with the mode of goodness, the false pride of considering oneself the Lord should certainly not affect them. When I break the false prestige of those bereft of goodness, My purpose is to bring them relief.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान्मच्छरणं गोष्ठं मन्नाथं मत्परिग्रहम् । गोपाये स्वात्मयोगेन सोऽयं मे व्रत आहित: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmān mac-charaṇaṁ goṣṭhaṁman-nāthaṁ mat-parigrahamgopāye svātma-yogenaso ’yaṁ me vrata āhitaḥ
verses.text: I must therefore protect the cowherd community by My transcendental potency, for I am their shelter, I am their master, and indeed they are My own family. After all, I have taken a vow to protect My devotees.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वैकेन हस्तेन कृत्वा गोवर्धनाचलम् । दधार लीलया विष्णुश्छत्राकमिव बालक: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktvaikena hastenakṛtvā govardhanācalamdadhāra līlayā viṣṇuśchatrākam iva bālakaḥ
verses.text: Having said this, Lord Kṛṣṇa, who is Viṣṇu Himself, picked up Govardhana Hill with one hand and held it aloft just as easily as a child holds up a mushroom.

verses.sanskrit: अथाह भगवान् गोपान्हेऽम्ब तात व्रजौकस: । यथोपजोषं विशत गिरिगर्तं सगोधना: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: athāha bhagavān gopānhe ’mba tāta vrajaukasaḥyathopajoṣaṁ viśatagiri-gartaṁ sa-go-dhanāḥ
verses.text: The Lord then addressed the cowherd community: O Mother, O Father, O residents of Vraja, if you wish you may now come under this hill with your cows.

verses.sanskrit: न त्रास इह व: कार्यो मद्धस्ताद्रिनिपातनात् । वातवर्षभयेनालं तत्‍त्राणं विहितं हि व: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: na trāsa iha vaḥ kāryomad-dhastādri-nipātanātvāta-varṣa-bhayenālaṁtat-trāṇaṁ vihitaṁ hi vaḥ
verses.text: You should have no fear that this mountain will fall from My hand. And don’t be afraid of the wind and rain, for your deliverance from these afflictions has already been arranged.

verses.sanskrit: तथा निर्विविशुर्गर्तं कृष्णाश्वासितमानस: । यथावकाशं सधना: सव्रजा: सोपजीविन: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathā nirviviśur gartaṁkṛṣṇāśvāsita-mānasaḥyathāvakāśaṁ sa-dhanāḥsa-vrajāḥ sopajīvinaḥ
verses.text: Their minds thus pacified by Lord Kṛṣṇa, they all entered beneath the hill, where they found ample room for themselves and all their cows, wagons, servants and priests, and for all other members of the community as well.

verses.sanskrit: क्षुत्तृड्‍‌‌‌व्यथां सुखापेक्षां हित्वा तैर्व्रजवासिभि: । वीक्ष्यमाणो दधाराद्रिं सप्ताहं नाचलत् पदात् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṣut-tṛḍ-vyathāṁ sukhāpekṣāṁhitvā tair vraja-vāsibhiḥvīkṣyamāṇo dadhārādriṁsaptāhaṁ nācalat padāt
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa, forgetting hunger and thirst and putting aside all considerations of personal pleasure, stood there holding up the hill for seven days as the people of Vraja gazed upon Him.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्णयोगानुभावं तं निशम्येन्द्रोऽतिविस्मित: । निस्तम्भो भ्रष्टसङ्कल्प: स्वान्मेघान् सन्न्यवारयत् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇa-yogānubhāvaṁ taṁniśamyendro ’ti-vismitaḥnistambho bhraṣṭa-saṅkalpaḥsvān meghān sannyavārayat
verses.text: When Indra observed this exhibition of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s mystic power, he became most astonished. Pulled down from his platform of false pride, and his intentions thwarted, he ordered his clouds to desist.

verses.sanskrit: खं व्यभ्रमुदितादित्यं वातवर्षं च दारुणम् । निशम्योपरतं गोपान् गोवर्धनधरोऽब्रवीत् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: khaṁ vyabhram uditādityaṁvāta-varṣaṁ ca dāruṇamniśamyoparataṁ gopāngovardhana-dharo ’bravīt
verses.text: Seeing that the fierce wind and rain had now ceased, the sky had become clear of rainclouds, and the sun had risen, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the lifter of Govardhana Hill, spoke to the cowherd community as follows.

verses.sanskrit: निर्यात त्यजत त्रासं गोपा: सस्त्रीधनार्भका: । उपारतं वातवर्षं व्युदप्रायाश्च निम्नगा: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: niryāta tyajata trāsaṁgopāḥ sa-strī-dhanārbhakāḥupārataṁ vāta-varṣaṁvyuda-prāyāś ca nimnagāḥ
verses.text: [Lord Kṛṣṇa said:] My dear cowherd men, please go out with your wives, children and possessions. Give up your fear. The wind and rain have stopped, and the rivers’ high waters have subsided.

verses.sanskrit: ततस्ते निर्ययुर्गोपा: स्वं स्वमादाय गोधनम् । शकटोढोपकरणं स्त्रीबालस्थविरा: शनै: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatas te niryayur gopāḥsvaṁ svam ādāya go-dhanamśakaṭoḍhopakaraṇaṁstrī-bāla-sthavirāḥ śanaiḥ
verses.text: After collecting their respective cows and loading their paraphernalia into their wagons, the cowherd men went out. The women, children and elderly persons gradually followed them.

verses.sanskrit: भगवानपि तं शैलं स्वस्थाने पूर्ववत् प्रभु: । पश्यतां सर्वभूतानां स्थापयामास लीलया ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavān api taṁ śailaṁsva-sthāne pūrva-vat prabhuḥpaśyatāṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁsthāpayām āsa līlayā
verses.text: While all living creatures looked on, the Supreme Personality of Godhead put down the hill in its original place, just as it had stood before.

verses.sanskrit: तं प्रेमवेगान्निर्भृता व्रजौकसोयथा समीयु: परिरम्भणादिभि: । गोप्यश्च सस्‍नेहमपूजयन् मुदादध्यक्षताद्भ‍िर्युयुजु: सदाशिष: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ prema-vegān nirbhṛtā vrajaukasoyathā samīyuḥ parirambhaṇādibhiḥgopyaś ca sa-sneham apūjayan mudādadhy-akṣatādbhir yuyujuḥ sad-āśiṣaḥ
verses.text: All the residents of Vṛndāvana were overwhelmed with ecstatic love, and they came forward and greeted Śrī Kṛṣṇa according to their individual relationships with Him — some embracing Him, others bowing down to Him, and so forth. The cowherd women presented water mixed with yogurt and unbroken barleycorns as a token of honor, and they showered auspicious benedictions upon Him.

verses.sanskrit: यशोदा रोहिणी नन्दो रामश्च बलिनां वर: । कृष्णमालिङ्‌‌ग्य युयुजुराशिष: स्‍नेहकातरा: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: yaśodā rohiṇī nandorāmaś ca balināṁ varaḥkṛṣṇam āliṅgya yuyujurāśiṣaḥ sneha-kātarāḥ
verses.text: Mother Yaśodā, mother Rohiṇī, Nanda Mahārāja and Balarāma, the greatest of the strong, all embraced Kṛṣṇa. Overwhelmed with affection, they offered Him their blessings.

verses.sanskrit: दिवि देवगणा: सिद्धा: साध्या गन्धर्वचारणा: । तुष्टुवुर्मुमुचुस्तुष्टा: पुष्पवर्षाणि पार्थिव ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: divi deva-gaṇāḥ siddhāḥsādhyā gandharva-cāraṇāḥtuṣṭuvur mumucus tuṣṭāḥpuṣpa-varṣāṇi pārthiva
verses.text: In the heavens, O King, all the demigods, including the Siddhas, Sādhyas, Gandharvas and Cāraṇas, sang the praises of Lord Kṛṣṇa and showered down flowers in great satisfaction.

verses.sanskrit: शङ्खदुन्दुभयो नेदुर्दिवि देवप्रचोदिता: । जगुर्गन्धर्वपतयस्तुम्बुरुप्रमुखा नृप ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śaṅkha-dundubhayo nedurdivi deva-pracoditāḥjagur gandharva-patayastumburu-pramukhā nṛpa
verses.text: My dear Parīkṣit, the demigods in heaven resoundingly played their conchshells and kettledrums, and the best of the Gandharvas, led by Tumburu, began to sing.

verses.sanskrit: ततोऽनुरक्तै: पशुपै: परिश्रितोराजन् स्वगोष्ठं सबलोऽव्रजद्धरि: । तथाविधान्यस्य कृतानि गोपिकागायन्त्य ईयुर्मुदिता हृदिस्पृश: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato ’nuraktaiḥ paśupaiḥ pariśritorājan sva-goṣṭhaṁ sa-balo ’vrajad dhariḥtathā-vidhāny asya kṛtāni gopikāgāyantya īyur muditā hṛdi-spṛśaḥ
verses.text: Surrounded by His loving cowherd boyfriends and Lord Balarāma, Kṛṣṇa then went off to the place where He had been tending His cows. The cowherd girls returned to their homes, singing joyfully about the lifting of Govardhana Hill and other glorious deeds performed by Lord Kṛṣṇa, who had so deeply touched their hearts.
